articles.full_text:
Odisha to Witness Three Heatwave Days This Week, Says IMD
Odisha is set to experience intense heat this week as the India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast heatwave conditions for three days in several parts of the state. The maximum temperature is expected to rise by 4°C to 6°C over the next four to five days.
According to the forecast, Bargarh and Balangir may witness heatwave conditions on Tuesday, while Bargarh, Balangir, Jharsuguda, and Sambalpur are likely to remain under heatwave alert on Wednesday and Thursday. Meanwhile, hot and humid weather is expected to prevail in districts including Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapara, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Puri, Khurda, Nayagarh, Ganjam, and Gajapati till Saturday.
The Special Relief Commissioner’s office has advised all district collectors to urge people to avoid stepping out between 11 am and 3:30 pm unless necessary and to take precautionary measures during the ongoing sultry conditions.
On Sunday, temperatures continued to rise across Odisha, with six places recording 40°C or above. Titilagarh was the hottest at 41.5°C, followed by Bhawanipatna and Balangir at 41°C each, Jharsuguda at 40.7°C, Sambalpur at 40.1°C, and Hirakud at 40°C.
